title: Central Bank Burns Through $800 Million Defending Rial
detail: The Central Bank of Iran has spent an estimated $800 million in foreign exchange reserves over the past week attempting to defend the rial, which is approaching 1,400,000 per dollar. Traders report persistent sell pressure despite the intervention, driven by sanctions enforcement, capital flight, and the psychological impact of repeated record lows. Economists warn the reserve burn rate is unsustainable, as Iran's accessible reserves have been severely depleted by sanctions and the costs of the June war.

title: IRGC Signs $589 Million Missile Acquisition Deal
detail: Intelligence reports indicate the Islamic Republic signed a secret deal valued at $589 million to acquire advanced shoulder-fired missiles and air defense components, as the conventional armed forces struggle to rebuild capabilities destroyed in the June war. The procurement, believed to involve intermediaries in multiple countries, is part of Iran's broader effort to reconstitute its missile program. The deal's existence further inflames domestic criticism of military spending amid economic collapse.

title: Transport Costs Surge as Fuel Hike Cascades Through Economy
detail: Three days after the gasoline price increase, its effects ripple through the Iranian economy. Intercity freight costs rise 25-35%, pushing up the price of produce and consumer goods in urban markets. Public transportation fares increase in multiple cities despite government assurances of subsidized transit. The cascading costs compound the impact of the rial's decline and the elimination of the preferential exchange rate, creating a triple squeeze on household purchasing power.
